prompts: Shanghai experiences a subtropical monsoon climate characterized by hot, humid summers and mild winters. The southeast wind that blows during summer is part of the East Asian Monsoon, driven by differential heating between the Asian continent and the Pacific Ocean. In summer, the landmass heats up more than the ocean, creating a low-pressure area over the continent. Air moves from the high-pressure area over the ocean towards the low-pressure area on land, bringing moist, warm air that results in the southeast monsoon winds and significant summer rainfall in Shanghai.
